Here are the findings:

---

## 1. ScanDefaults model – DuplexMode field

**File:** `d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Models\ApiModels.cs`

`ScanDefaults` has a `DuplexMode` property:

```111:118:d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Models\ApiModels.cs
    public class ScanDefaults
    {
        public int Dpi { get; set; } = 300;
        public string ColorMode { get; set; } = "Grayscale";
        public string PageSize { get; set; } = "A4";
        public string DuplexMode { get; set; } = "Simplex";
        public string ImageFormat { get; set; } = "jpeg";
        public int JpegQuality { get; set; } = 85;
    }
```

---

## 2. WorkstationInfo – DriverType, PrinterProfileID, TwainCapabilities

**File:** `d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Models\ApiModels.cs`

```93:108:d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Models\ApiModels.cs
    public class WorkstationInfo
    {
        public int WorkstationID { get; set; }
        public string WorkstationCode { get; set; } = "";
        public string WorkstationName { get; set; } = "";
        public string? AssignedUsername { get; set; }
        public int? PrinterProfileID { get; set; }
        public string? PrinterProfileName { get; set; }
        public string? PrinterBrand { get; set; }
        public string? DriverType { get; set; }
        public string? TwainCapabilities { get; set; }
        public int? LocationID { get; set; }
        public string? LocationCode { get; set; }
        public string? LocationName { get; set; }

        public override string ToString() => $"{WorkstationCode} — {WorkstationName}";
    }
```

- **DriverType** – `string?`, e.g. `"WIA"` or `"TWAIN"`.
- **PrinterProfileID** – `int?`, links to a `PrinterProfile` for TWAIN.
- **TwainCapabilities** – `string?`, JSON used for TWAIN capability overrides.

`PrinterProfile` also has `TwainCapabilities` and a helper:

```138:161:d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Models\ApiModels.cs
    public class PrinterProfile
    {
        public int ProfileID { get; set; }
        public string ProfileName { get; set; } = "";
        public string Brand { get; set; } = "Generic";
        public string DriverType { get; set; } = "WIA";
        /// <summary>JSON object mapping TWAIN CAP names to values.</summary>
        public string? TwainCapabilities { get; set; }

        public Dictionary<string, object> GetCapabilities()
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(TwainCapabilities))
                return new Dictionary<string, object>();
            try
            {
                return Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<Dictionary<string, object>>(TwainCapabilities)
                       ?? new Dictionary<string, object>();
            }
            catch { return new Dictionary<string, object>(); }
        }

        public override string ToString() => $"{ProfileName} [{DriverType}]";
    }
```

`TwainScannerService` uses `PrinterProfile.TwainCapabilities` for TWAIN CAPs (e.g. `ICAP_XRESOLUTION`, `CAP_DUPLEXENABLED`).

---

## 3. MainForm – ApplyWorkstation (WIA vs TWAIN)

**File:** `d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Forms\MainForm.cs`

```557:589:d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Forms\MainForm.cs
        private void ApplyWorkstation()
        {
            if (_myWorkstation == null)
            {
                _lblWorkstation.Text = "No workstation assigned";
                _lblWorkstation.ForeColor = ColorDanger;
                return;
            }

            _lblWorkstation.Text      = $"{_myWorkstation.WorkstationCode} — {_myWorkstation.WorkstationName}";
            _lblWorkstation.ForeColor = ColorPrimary;

            var driverType = _myWorkstation.DriverType ?? "WIA";
            _lblDriverMode.Text = $"Driver: {driverType}  |  Printer: {_myWorkstation.PrinterProfileName ?? "Default"}";

            // Swap scanner implementation if TWAIN printer profile is assigned
            if (driverType.Equals("TWAIN", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                && _myWorkstation.PrinterProfileID.HasValue)
            {
                var profile = _settings?.PrinterProfiles
                    .FirstOrDefault(p => p.ProfileID == _myWorkstation.PrinterProfileID.Value);

                _scanner = new TwainScannerService(profile);
                _lblDriverMode.ForeColor = Color.FromArgb(160, 100, 20);
            }
            else
            {
                _scanner = new ScannerService();
                _lblDriverMode.ForeColor = ColorAccent;
            }

            // Update form title
            Text = $"Scanner — {_myWorkstation.WorkstationCode}";
        }
```

Logic:

1. Default driver is `"WIA"` if `DriverType` is null.
2. If `DriverType` is `"TWAIN"` (case-insensitive) **and** `PrinterProfileID` is set:
   - Look up `PrinterProfile` by `ProfileID`
   - Use `TwainScannerService(profile)`
3. Otherwise use `ScannerService()` (WIA).

---

## 4. ScanCompleteDialog – purpose and when it’s shown

**File:** `d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Forms\ScanCompleteDialog.cs`

Purpose: modal dialog shown after a booklet scan finishes. It shows barcode details, page 1 thumbnail, page count, and actions.

```6:11:d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Forms\ScanCompleteDialog.cs
    /// <summary>
    /// Modal popup shown after a booklet is fully scanned.
    /// Displays barcode details decoded from page 1, a thumbnail of page 1,
    /// and offers "Save & Queue Upload" or "Discard" actions.
    /// </summary>
    public class ScanCompleteDialog : Form
```

Buttons:

- **Save & Queue Upload** – `DialogResult.OK`, `SaveRequested = true`
- **Discard** – `DialogResult.Cancel`, `SaveRequested = false`
- **Re-scan** – `DialogResult.Retry`, `SaveRequested = false`

It is shown in `BtnScan_Click` right after pages are saved to disk and the folder is renamed (see section 7).

---

## 5. PDF-related NuGet packages

There are no PDF-related NuGet packages in the project. The `ScannerApp` folder has no references to PDF, iText, PdfSharp, QuestPDF, etc.

- Newtonsoft.Json

## 7. BtnScan_Click – scan complete flow, saving, ScanCompleteDialog

**File:** `d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Forms\MainForm.cs`

Flow:

1. **Scan** – `_scanner.ScanBookletAsync(...)` returns `bitmaps`.
2. **Save to disk** – create `PENDING` folder, save each page as `page_001.jpg`, etc., and build `_currentPages`.
3. **Barcode** – decode from page 1, build `BarcodeDetails`.
4. **Rename folder** – move from `PENDING` to `booklets\{bookletId}`.
5. **Show UI** – `PopulatePageList(bitmaps, barcodeDetails)`.
6. **Show ScanCompleteDialog** – modal with barcode, thumbnails, and actions.
7. **Handle result:**
   - **OK + SaveRequested** – create `LocalBookletRecord`, call `_queue.SaveToQueue(record)`, refresh queue, trigger upload.
   - **Retry** – delete folder, allow re-scan.
   - **Cancel** – delete folder, discard.

```696:793:d:\Scanning&Evaluation\scanner-desktop\ScannerApp\Forms\MainForm.cs
                // Save pages to disk
                var folder = BuildBookletFolder("PENDING", effectiveTemplate);
                Directory.CreateDirectory(folder);

                for (int i = 0; i < bitmaps.Count; i++)
                {
                    int pageNum = i + 1;
                    var imagePath = Path.Combine(folder, Path.Combine(folder, $"page_{pageNum:D3}.jpg");
                    ImageHelper.SaveAsJpeg(bitmaps[i], imagePath, effectiveTemplate.JpegQuality);
                    // ... _currentPages.Add(sp);
                }

                // Decode barcode from page 1
                var barcodeDetails = BarcodeDetails.Parse("");
                // ... try { barcodeDetails = BarcodeDetails.Parse(rawBarcode ?? ""); }

                // Rename folder to final booklet ID
                var bookletId = barcodeDetails.ToFilename() + $"_{DateTime.Now:HHmmss}";
                var finalFolder = Path.Combine(_storagePath, "booklets", bookletId);
                // ... Directory.Move(folder, finalFolder);

                // Show in page list
                PopulatePageList(bitmaps, barcodeDetails);

                // Show ScanCompleteDialog
                using var dlg = new ScanCompleteDialog(barcodeDetails, bitmaps, effectiveTemplate.PageCount);
                var result = dlg.ShowDialog(this);

                if (result == DialogResult.OK && dlg.SaveRequested)
                {
                    // Build queue record, _queue?.SaveToQueue(record), RefreshQueueView(), TryUploadPendingAsync()
                    SetStatus($"Saved: {bookletId} ({bitmaps.Count} pages)", false);
                }
                else if (result == DialogResult.Retry)
                {
                    try { if (Directory.Exists(finalFolder)) Directory.Delete(finalFolder, true); } catch { }
                    SetStatus("Ready for re-scan", false);
                }
                else
                {
                    try { if (Directory.Exists(finalFolder)) Directory.Delete(finalFolder, true); } catch { }
                    SetStatus("Scan discarded", false);
                }
```

Important: pages are saved to disk **before** the dialog is shown. The dialog only controls whether the booklet is queued for upload, discarded, or re-scanned.
